mission.title: Install and Configure SurrealDB
mission.description: SurrealDB is a scalable, distributed, collaborative, document-graph database designed for the realtime web. It is ideal for applications requiring flexible data models and real-time capabilities. Key trade-offs include complexity in setup and management compared to traditional databases.

steps.title: Add the SurrealDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official SurrealDB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add surrealdb https://charts.surrealdb.com

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for SurrealDB.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install SurrealDB with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs SurrealDB in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surreal --create-namespace --version 3.0.2

```

> Installs SurrealDB in the 'surreal' namespace.

steps.title: Create a sample configuration
steps.description:
This step creates a sample configuration for SurrealDB to ensure it runs with the desired settings.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: surrealdb-sample
  namespace: surreal
spec:
  db: test_db
EOF

```

> Creates a sample database configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: surrealdb-sample
  namespace: surreal
spec:
  db: test_db
EOF

steps.title: Verify SurrealDB installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the SurrealDB pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n surreal

```

> Lists all pods in the 'surreal' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall SurrealDB
uninstall.description:
This step removes the SurrealDB installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall surrealdb --namespace surreal

kubectl delete namespace surreal

```

> Uninstalls the SurrealDB release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade SurrealDB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade SurrealDB to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl exec -it <surrealdb-pod-name> -- surrealdb backup --output /backup

helm upgrade surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surreal --version 3.0.2

kubectl get pods -n surreal

```

> Backup your current database.
> Upgrades SurrealDB to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the SurrealDB service is not running or the connection parameters are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n surreal' and ensure the service is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB pod may not have enough resources allocated to it.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the Helm chart values and redeploy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Database not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified database may not exist or was not created properly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the database is created using the correct configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the RBAC settings are not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the RBAC settings and ensure the service account has the necessary permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for multi-model queries and improved performance for concurrent connections.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed legacy API endpoints for database access.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.x, ensure to migrate your database schema to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the SurrealDB pods running with 'STATUS' as 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n surreal'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
